zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth於1330年出生在英國蘇格蘭洛包爾時，他的父親John Gray Lord of Broxmouth26歲，母親Alice de L'Isle24歲。他和Joanne de Cobham至少有1個兒子。他去世於1413年12月19日在他的家鄉享年83歲。

nameMeanings.name: Gray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: McGreevy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Seamus, Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Riabhaigh, a patronymic from the personal name Riabhach ‘brindled, grizzled’. Compare Reavey and Creevy 2.

variants.name: Grayer
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English grei ‘gray’ (see Gray ) + her ‘hair’.

variants.name: Grays
meanings: English (Durham): variant of Gray , with post-medieval excrescent -s, or of Grace .

variants.name: Grice
meanings: English (Midlands): from Middle English grice, grise ‘pig’ (Old Norse gríss), probably a nickname or a metonymic occupational name for a swineherd.
English: nickname for a gray-haired man, from Middle English grice, gris ‘gray’ (Old French gris, apparently of ancient Germanic origin, and probably a distant cognate of Gray 1).
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Greis  .

variants.name: Grey
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

nameMeanings.name: Patrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Name of the apostle and patron saint of Ireland ( c.389–461 ), Gaelic Pádraig. He was a Christian Briton and a Roman citizen, who as a young man was captured and enslaved by raiders from Ireland. He escaped and went to Gaul before returning home to Britain. In about 419 he felt a call to do missionary work in Ireland. He studied for twelve years at Auxerre, and in 432 returned to Ireland. For the rest of his life it is difficult to distinguish fact from fiction. He apparently went to the court of the high kings at Tara and made some converts there; then he travelled around Ireland making further converts until about 445 , when he established his archiepiscopal see at Armagh. By the time of his death almost the whole of Ireland is said to have been converted to Christianity. He is also credited with codifying the laws of Ireland. In his Latin autobiography, as well as in later tradition, his name appears as Patricius ‘patrician’ (i.e. belonging to the Roman senatorial or noble class), but this may actually represent a Latinized form of some lost Celtic (British) name. In Ireland in particular, it has been one of the most enduringly popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth was born in 1330, in Roxburghshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, John Gray Lord of Broxmouth, was 26 and his mother, Alice de L'Isle, was 24. He had at least 1 son with Joanne de Cobham. He died on 19 December 1413, in his hometown, at the age of 83.

Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Joanne de Cobham. Il est décédé le 19 décembre 1413, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans.

Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Joanne de Cobham. Morì il 19 dicembre 1413, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 83 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouthが1330年にイギリススコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアで生まれた時，父John Gray Lord of Broxmouthは26歳，母Alice de L'Isleは24歳であった。彼はJoanne de Cobhamとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1413年12月19日に故郷で83歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth(이)가 1330년 영국 스코틀랜드 록스버그h실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Gray Lord of Broxmouth(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de L'Isle(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Joanne de Cobham(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1413년 12월 19일 그의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth родился в 1330 г. в местности роксбургшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Gray Lord of Broxmouth было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de L'Isle, 24. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Joanne de Cobham. Он умер 19 декабря 1413 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 83 лет.
